tasks.title: Hide unusable action buttons in updateUI()
tasks.description: Modify the updateUI() function in game.js to set style.display = 'none' on action buttons when their corresponding canBuild/canHarvest check fails, and restore display when the check passes. Add a 'No actions available' fallback message when all buttons are hidden.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
1. Action buttons (gather, build hovel, build solar panel, build battery, build rocktimus, build comm dish) are hidden when the player lacks resources/preconditions.
2. Buttons reappear immediately when preconditions are met (e.g., after gathering rocks).
3. Gather button is hidden when selected unit is not on a harvestable resource or has no moves left.
4. A contextual message or the end turn button remains visible at all times so the UI is never empty.
5. All hotkeys continue to function as no-ops when preconditions aren't met, matching current disabled behavior.
tasks.claudeMd:
# Project Context You are modifying a vanilla HTML/CSS/JS browser game (no framework, no build system). The entire game logic lives in `game.js` (~1400 lines). The UI is defined in `index.html` with styles in `style.css`. ## What to Change Modify the `updateUI()` function at **game.js:694-742**. For each action button, add a `style.display` line alongside the existing `disabled` assignment. ### Pattern The existing code looks like: ```js const gatherBtn = document.getElementById("gather-btn"); gatherBtn.disabled = !canUnitHarvest(unit); ``` Change to: ```js const gatherBtn = document.getElementById("gather-btn"); const canGather = canUnitHarvest(unit); gatherBtn.disabled = !canGather; gatherBtn.style.display = canGather ? "" : "none"; ``` Apply this same pattern to all 6 action buttons: - `gather-btn` — uses `canUnitHarvest(unit)` (note: `canUnitHarvest` also checks `unit.movesLeft` is irrelevant — it checks tile resource and unit type; however the gather action itself in `gatherResource()` does NOT check moves. The disabled check in updateUI is just `canUnitHarvest(unit)`. Keep this as-is.) - `build-hovel-btn` — uses `state.resources.rocks >= 10 && !hasStructureOnTile` - `build-solar-btn` — uses `canBuildSolarPanel()` - `build-battery-btn` — uses `canBuildSubparBattery()` - `build-rocktimus-btn` — uses `canBuildRocktimus()` - `build-comm-dish-btn` — uses `canBuildCommDish()` ### Fallback Message Add a `
` element in `index.html` inside the `#action-panel`, right after the last button. Style it as a subtle text message like the existing `#tile-details`. In `updateUI()`, after setting all button visibility, check if ALL buttons are hidden — if so, show the message; otherwise hide it. Example HTML to add inside `#action-panel` after the last button: ```html
No actions available — move to a resource or gain more resources.
``` Example JS at end of button visibility block in updateUI(): ```js var noActionsMsg = document.getElementById("no-actions-msg"); var anyVisible = canGather || canHovel || canSolar || canBattery || canRock || canComm; noActionsMsg.style.display = anyVisible ? "none" : ""; ``` ### CSS for no-actions message Add to `style.css`: ```css .no-actions-msg { font-size: 0.8rem; color: #aa9988; padding: 8px 0; text-align: center; } ``` ## DO NOT Change - Do NOT modify the hotkey handler (keydown listener around line 1406). Hotkeys call action functions directly, and each function has its own guard clause. They are unaffected by button visibility. - Do NOT modify the click event listeners (lines 1378-1402). They call the action functions which have guard clauses. - Do NOT remove the `disabled` property assignments — keep them alongside the new `style.display` lines. - Do NOT modify the `canBuild*` or `canUnitHarvest` functions. ## Conventions - Use `var` for variable declarations to match existing code style (the file mixes `var`/`const`/`let` — use `var` for new locals unless matching adjacent `const` usage) - No semicolons are inconsistently used in the file — match the surrounding code's style - Test by opening `index.html` in a browser — verify buttons appear/disappear as you gather resources and move around ## Adjacent Sibling Margin The CSS rule `#action-panel .action-btn + .action-btn { margin-top: 6px; }` uses the adjacent sibling combinator. When a button is `display: none`, it's removed from the flow, so the `+` combinator naturally skips it — no CSS fix needed for spacing.

checklist.description: Gather button is hidden when unit cannot harvest (no resource on tile or unit type can't harvest)
steps: Read game.js and locate the gather button logic in updateUI() around line 716-717
Capture canUnitHarvest(unit) result into a variable
Add gatherBtn.style.display = canGather ? '' : 'none' after the disabled line
Verify by opening in browser: move unit to empty tile, gather button should disappear; move to rock tile, it should reappear

checklist.description: Build Rock Hovel button is hidden when rocks < 10 or structure already on tile
steps: Read game.js and locate the build hovel button logic in updateUI() around line 720-722
Capture the canBuild condition (rocks >= 10 && !hasStructureOnTile) into a variable
Add buildBtn.style.display = canHovel ? '' : 'none' after the disabled line
Verify: with < 10 rocks, button is hidden; gather 10+ rocks on empty tile, button appears

checklist.description: Build Solar Panel button is hidden when canBuildSolarPanel() returns false
steps: Read game.js and locate the solar panel button logic in updateUI() around line 725-726
Capture canBuildSolarPanel() result into a variable
Add solarBtn.style.display = canSolar ? '' : 'none' after the disabled line
Verify: button hidden when not adjacent to hovel or insufficient rocks; appears when conditions met

checklist.description: Build Battery, Rocktimus, and Comm Dish buttons are hidden when their can* checks fail
steps: Apply the same pattern to build-battery-btn (canBuildSubparBattery), build-rocktimus-btn (canBuildRocktimus), and build-comm-dish-btn (canBuildCommDish)
Capture each can* result into a variable, add style.display toggle
Verify each button appears/disappears based on resource and position preconditions

checklist.description: Add 'No actions available' fallback message in action panel when all buttons hidden
steps: Add <div id='no-actions-msg'> to index.html inside #action-panel after the last button
Add CSS class .no-actions-msg in style.css with subtle styling
In updateUI(), after all button visibility is set, check if any button is visible
Show/hide the no-actions-msg based on whether any action is available
Verify: when no actions available, message shows; when any action available, message hidden

checklist.description: Hotkeys remain functional as no-ops when buttons are hidden
steps: Verify that the keydown handler (around line 1406) calls action functions directly without checking button DOM state
Confirm each action function has its own guard clause (canBuild*/canUnitHarvest check)
Test: press G when not on a resource tile — should be a no-op (no error, no action)
Test: press B when rocks < 10 — should be a no-op

quality_checklist.description: No duplicate DOM lookups — each getElementById call appears once in updateUI()
steps: Review updateUI() and confirm each button element is fetched once per call
Ensure no redundant getElementById calls were introduced

quality_checklist.description: disabled property is still set alongside display — both are present for all 6 buttons
steps: Check that every action button still has .disabled = !canX assignment
Verify .style.display is set right after .disabled for each button

quality_checklist.description: No unnecessary code changes outside updateUI() and the two new elements (HTML div, CSS class)
steps: Review the full diff and confirm changes are limited to: updateUI() in game.js, one new div in index.html, one new CSS class in style.css
Ensure hotkey handler, click listeners, and can* functions are untouched

quality_checklist.description: Variable naming follows existing conventions in updateUI()
steps: Check that new variable names (canGather, canHovel, etc.) are consistent in style
Verify var/const usage matches surrounding code

checks.description: Action buttons are hidden when preconditions are not met at game start
steps: Start the app and open in browser
At game start with 0 rocks and 0 energy, verify that build hovel, build solar, build battery, build rocktimus, and build comm dish buttons are NOT visible
Verify the gather button is visible only if Elon starts on a resource tile
Verify the 'No actions available' message appears if no buttons are visible
Move Elon to a tile with rocks and gather
After gathering enough rocks (10+), move to an empty tile
Verify the Build Rock Hovel button appears
Verify it disappears again after building a hovel (rocks drop below 10)

checks.description: Gather button visibility tracks tile resource presence
steps: Move Elon to a tile with rocks — gather button should be visible
Move Elon to an empty tile — gather button should be hidden
Move back to a rock tile — gather button should reappear

checks.description: Hotkeys work as no-ops when buttons are hidden
steps: With no resources, press B (build hovel) — nothing should happen, no errors in console
Press G on an empty tile — nothing should happen
Press P (solar panel) with no hovel nearby — nothing should happen

checks.description: End turn button is always visible
steps: In any game state, verify the End Turn button is always visible in the sidebar
After hiding all action buttons, verify the UI is not completely empty — end turn and/or no-actions message visible
